identity.name: WayofDojo - The Digital Dojo
principles: Fusion: Ancient Tradition x Cyberpunk Progression
Emotion: The journey from white belt to black belt is a hero's journey
Contrast: Dark void backgrounds vs. Neon energy of knowledge
Density: Information-rich but scannable (like a fighter jet HUD)

headings.usage: Section titles, Rank names, Achievement unlocks

usage_rules.gradients: Use subtle linear gradients for surfaces (e.g., slate-900 to slate-950). NO full-screen rainbow gradients.
usage_rules.glows: Use box-shadow for 'energy' effects on active elements.
usage_rules.borders: 1px solid borders with low opacity (white/10) are mandatory for all cards.

textures.noise: url('data:image/svg+xml;base64,...') (Use a standard noise SVG pattern)

layout_strategies.grid_system: Bento Grid for dashboards, Constellation Layout for technique trees.
layout_strategies.spacing: Generous padding (p-6, p-8) inside containers. Tight gaps (gap-2, gap-4) between related data points.
layout_strategies.container: Max-width 1400px for the main library view.

TechniqueCard.description: A card representing a single technique. Shows name, category icon, and mastery status.
TechniqueCard.style: Glassmorphic surface, neon border on hover. 'Locked' state if not yet accessible.
TechniqueCard.interactions: Click to open details modal. Hover to see quick stats (XP value).

ProgressionBar.description: Visual indicator of progress towards the next belt/rank.
ProgressionBar.style: Slim, glowing bar. Segmented by belt requirements.

FilterConsole.description: Cyberpunk-style toggle switches for filtering techniques (Nage, Osae, Weapons).
FilterConsole.style: Pill-shaped toggles, active state glows with primary color.

MasteryBadge.style: Metallic gradient (Bronze, Silver, Gold) with inner glow.

images.description: Dark Japanese paper texture for background overlay
Martial arts silhouette for section headers or empty states

assets.icons: Use Lucide React icons. Map categories to icons: 'Circle' for Ukemi, 'Wind' for Tai Sabaki, 'Hand' for Nage, 'Shield' for Osae, 'Sword' for Weapons.

instructions_to_main_agent.step_1: Setup the global theme variables in Tailwind config if not present (add custom colors).
instructions_to_main_agent.step_2: Create the 'TechniqueLibrary' container component. Use the 'Japanese Paper' texture as a subtle background blend.
instructions_to_main_agent.step_3: Implement the 'FilterConsole' at the top. It should feel like a spaceship control panel.
instructions_to_main_agent.step_4: Build the 'TechniqueGrid'. Use a responsive grid (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4).
instructions_to_main_agent.step_5: Create the 'TechniqueCard' component. Ensure it handles 'locked', 'available', and 'mastered' states visually.
instructions_to_main_agent.step_6: Add the 'GamificationLayer': XP progress bars, floating '+XP' text on interaction (using Sonner or Framer Motion).
instructions_to_main_agent.step_7: Ensure all text is legible against the dark background. Use 'text-slate-300' for body and 'text-white' for headings.

do_not: Do NOT use default scrollbars. Custom thin scrollbars only.
Do NOT use pure black (#000000). Use Slate-950 (#020617).
Do NOT make the UI look like a spreadsheet. It must feel like a game interface.
must_do: MUST use 'data-testid' for all interactive elements.
MUST ensure mobile responsiveness (stack grids to 1 column).
MUST use 'backdrop-blur' for overlays to maintain context.
